title: Create and maintain HR and business policies
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to create and maintain HR and business policies, so that the organization has a documented, authoritative set of policies to govern operations.
acceptance_criteria: Given I have policy management rights, when I create a new policy with its title, content/body, and attributes, then the policy is saved and available for assignment.; Given a new policy, when I define both HR policies and business policies, then the system supports and stores each policy type distinctly.; Given an existing policy, when I edit its details (content or attributes), then the updated policy is retained and its prior state is preserved as a version; Given a policy I am maintaining, when I deactivate or retire it, then it is no longer applied to the population while its history remains available; Given I attempt to save a policy with missing mandatory fields, when I submit, then the system blocks the save and prompts for the required information.
source: FR 6.10.1 (creation and maintenance of HR and business policies)

title: Assign policies by organizational scope
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to assign policies based on company, jurisdiction, location, department, group, and employment type, so that each policy applies only to the intended population.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am configuring a policy, when I select one or more scoping attributes (company, jurisdiction, location, department, group, employment type), then the policy applies only to entities matching those attributes.; Given I combine multiple scoping attributes, when the policy is evaluated, then only entities matching all selected attributes inherit the policy.; Given a policy scoped to a specific department, when a user outside that department is evaluated, then the policy does not apply to them.; Given a policy scoped to an employment type, when a worker of a different employment type is evaluated, then the policy does not apply; Given overlapping policies apply to the same population, when applicability is resolved, then the system deterministically selects the applicable policy per its scope and effective dating.
source: FR 6.10.1 (assignable by company, jurisdiction, location, department, group, employment type)

title: Assign policies by jurisdiction
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to assign policies by jurisdiction, so that legal and regulatory requirements specific to each jurisdiction are correctly enforced.
acceptance_criteria: Given entities operating in different jurisdictions, when I assign a policy to a jurisdiction, then only entities in that jurisdiction inherit the policy.; Given a jurisdiction-scoped policy, when I review applicability, then the system reflects the correct jurisdiction mapping.; Given an employee whose jurisdiction changes, when applicability is re-evaluated, then the jurisdiction-appropriate policy is applied.
source: FR 6.10.1 (jurisdiction-based assignment)

title: Version policies
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want policies to support versioning, so that I can update policy content over time while preserving a history of prior versions.
acceptance_criteria: Given an existing policy, when I make changes and save, then a new version is created while previous versions are retained.; Given multiple versions of a policy, when I review the policy, then I can identify the current version and all historical versions with their change details.; Given a superseded version, when I open it, then I can view its content read-only for audit and reference; Given a new version is created, when it is not yet in force, then the prior version remains the applicable one until the new version's effective date.
source: FR 6.10.2 (versioning)

title: Apply effective dating to policies
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to set effective dates on policy versions, so that a policy takes effect at the correct time and superseded versions are applied only for their valid periods.
acceptance_criteria: Given a policy version with an effective date, when the current date is before that date, then the version is not yet in force.; Given a policy version with an effective date, when that date is reached, then the version becomes the applicable one for its scope and supersedes the prior version.; Given a policy with a series of dated versions, when applicability is evaluated for any date, then the version in force on that date is applied; Given I set an effective date, when it conflicts with an existing version's active period, then the system enforces non-overlapping effective periods.
source: FR 6.10.2 (effective dating)

title: Differentiate applicability for employees and contractors
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want policies to have different applicability for employees and contractors, so that each worker type is governed by the rules appropriate to their engagement.
acceptance_criteria: Given a policy, when I configure applicability, then I can specify whether it applies to employees, contractors, or both.; Given a contractor-only policy, when an employee is evaluated, then the policy does not apply to that employee.; Given an employee-only policy, when a contractor is evaluated, then the policy does not apply to that contractor; Given a policy applying to both, when either worker type is evaluated, then the policy applies to both.
source: FR 6.10.2 (different applicability for employees and contractors)

title: Integrate policies with operational modules
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want policies to integrate with the leave, attendance, onboarding, probation, performance, exit, and asset management modules, so that policy rules are consistently enforced across HR processes.
acceptance_criteria: Given a policy relevant to a module (e.g. leave or attendance), when a process runs in that module, then the applicable policy is applied automatically.; Given policies linked to onboarding, probation, performance, exit, and asset management, when those processes execute, then they respect the associated policy rules.; Given a policy is updated to a new version, when a downstream module process runs after the effective date, then the module consumes the current in-force policy; Given a policy scope excludes a worker, when a module process runs for that worker, then the module does not apply the excluded policy.
source: FR 6.10.3 (integration with leave, attendance, onboarding, probation, performance, exit, asset management)

title: Role-controlled policy maintenance
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want policy maintenance to be role controlled, so that only authorized roles can create or modify policies.
acceptance_criteria: Given a user without policy maintenance rights, when they attempt to create or edit a policy, then the action is denied.; Given a user with the appropriate role, when they access policy maintenance, then they can create and modify policies within their scope.; Given role-based permissions are configured, when I define which roles may maintain policies, then the system enforces those permissions consistently across the platform; Given a maintenance action is performed, when it succeeds or is denied, then the event is auditable with the acting user and role.
source: FR 6.10.4 (role-controlled maintenance)

title: Role-controlled policy visibility
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want to see only the policies applicable to my role and scope, so that I view relevant policies without exposure to unrelated or restricted policies.
acceptance_criteria: Given policy visibility is role controlled, when I view policies, then I see only those my role and scope are permitted to see.; Given a policy outside my visibility scope, when I browse policies, then that policy is not shown to me.; Given multiple versions of a policy exist, when I view it, then I see only the version currently in force for my scope.
source: FR 6.10.4 (role-controlled visibility)

title: Oversee policies across group companies
user_story: As a Portfolio Admin, I want to view and govern policies across the group companies in my portfolio, so that policy standards are consistent and compliant across the whole portfolio.
acceptance_criteria: Given I administer a portfolio, when I access policy management, then I can view and maintain policies across all group companies within my portfolio scope.; Given a policy is scoped to a specific company, when I review portfolio-wide applicability, then the system reflects which companies inherit it.; Given a company outside my portfolio, when I attempt to access its policies, then access is denied per role control.
source: FR 6.10.1, FR 6.10.4 (company-scoped assignment; role-controlled maintenance)

title: Maintain policies for group companies
user_story: As a Group Company Admin, I want to create and maintain policies applicable to the companies within my group, so that group-level standards are enforced while respecting each company's scope.
acceptance_criteria: Given I administer a group of companies, when I create a policy scoped to the group, then it applies to the companies within that group.; Given I assign a policy at group level, when a company within the group is evaluated, then it inherits the group policy unless a more specific company-level policy overrides it.; Given a policy outside my group scope, when I attempt to maintain it, then the action is denied per role control.
source: FR 6.10.1, FR 6.10.4 (group-based assignment; role-controlled maintenance)

title: Governance of non-user workers by applicable policies
user_story: As an Employee (Non-User), I want the policies scoped to my company, jurisdiction, location, department, group, and employment type to apply to me even though I have no system login, so that I am governed by the correct rules through HR-managed processes.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am a non-user worker, when policies are evaluated for my company, jurisdiction, location, department, group, and employment type, then the applicable policies are applied to me by the system.; Given a policy differentiates by worker type, when I am classified as an employee or contractor, then the correct applicability is applied to me.; Given I have no login, when a policy applies, then it is surfaced and administered on my behalf by an authorized admin rather than through self-service.
source: FR 6.10.1, FR 6.10.2, FR 6.10.4 (scoped applicability; employee/contractor differentiation; role control)

title: Assign policies by employment type
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to assign policies specifically by employment type, so that full-time, part-time, contractor, and other engagement types receive the rules appropriate to them.
acceptance_criteria: Given multiple employment types exist, when I scope a policy to one or more employment types, then only workers of those types inherit the policy.; Given a worker's employment type changes, when applicability is re-evaluated, then the policies matching the new employment type are applied and those no longer matching are removed.; Given an employment-type-scoped policy, when I review applicability, then the system reflects the correct employment-type mapping.
source: FR 6.10.1 (employment-type assignment), FR 6.10.2 (employee/contractor differentiation)

title: Persist policies as effective-dated tenant-scoped records
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want policies and their versions persisted as effective-dated, tenant-scoped records with full history, so that every policy state is auditable and isolated per tenant.
acceptance_criteria: Given a policy version is created or superseded, when it is stored, then the system retains both the effective (business) period and the record's change history without overwriting prior states.; Given a query for any past date, when applicability is reconstructed, then the record in force on that date is returned from history.; Given multiple tenants, when policies are stored, then each policy is scoped to its owning tenant and row-level security prevents cross-tenant access; Given a policy is retired, when I inspect history, then all prior versions and their valid periods remain retrievable for audit.
source: FR 6.10.2 (L1 domain/data: effective-dated bitemporal history), FR 6.10.1

title: Enforce policy data integrity and non-overlapping periods
user_story: As a Platform Admin, I want the data model to enforce uniqueness and non-overlapping effective periods for policy versions, so that policy applicability is always deterministic and free of conflicting records.
acceptance_criteria: Given two versions of the same policy, when their effective periods would overlap, then the persistence layer rejects the second as an integrity violation.; Given a policy scoped to an applicability key (company, jurisdiction, location, department, group, employment type), when a duplicate active record for the same key and period is attempted, then the constraint prevents it.; Given a referential link to an organizational scope entity, when that entity is missing or invalid, then the policy record cannot be persisted.
source: FR 6.10.2 (L1 data integrity: non-overlapping versions), FR 6.10.1

title: Govern policy applicability as versioned configuration
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want policy applicability rules maintained as governed, versioned configuration that the platform engines read, so that I can change who a policy applies to without code changes.
acceptance_criteria: Given policy scoping and applicability, when I define them, then they are stored as per-tenant configuration (metadata) that engines consume at runtime rather than hard-coded logic.; Given I change the scoping configuration, when I save it, then a new configuration version with its own effective date is created and the prior configuration is preserved.; Given a configuration change, when its effective date is reached, then engines evaluate the new applicability without any deployment or code change; Given governed config, when I review it, then I can see which version is in force and its change lineage.
source: FR 6.10.1, FR 6.10.2 (L2 config: versioned, effective-dated governed metadata)

title: Resolve applicable policy via rules engine precedence
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want the shared rules engine to deterministically resolve which policy applies when multiple scoped policies overlap, so that the most specific applicable policy is selected consistently.
acceptance_criteria: Given multiple policies match a worker across company, group, location, department, jurisdiction, and employment type, when the rules engine evaluates applicability, then it selects a single policy using defined scope precedence where a more specific scope overrides a broader scope.; Given a group-level and a company-level policy both apply, when resolution runs, then the company-level policy overrides the group-level one.; Given effective dating, when resolution runs for a given date, then only versions in force on that date are considered; Given the same inputs, when the engine re-evaluates, then it returns the same deterministic result.
source: FR 6.10.1 (L3 rules engine: deterministic applicability resolution), FR 6.10.2

title: Notify affected population on policy changes
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want to be notified when a policy applicable to me is published or becomes effective, so that I am aware of the rules that govern me.
acceptance_criteria: Given a policy version becomes effective for my scope, when the notification engine runs, then I receive a notification generated from the platform's notification/template engine.; Given a policy is updated to a new version, when the change takes effect, then affected workers within the policy scope are notified while out-of-scope workers are not.; Given role-controlled visibility, when a notification is sent, then it respects my visibility permissions and does not disclose restricted policies.
source: FR 6.10.2, FR 6.10.4 (L3 notification engine: policy change alerts)

title: Author policies via metadata-driven authoring screen
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want a metadata-driven policy authoring screen with a dynamic scoping form, so that I can create and scope policies through a guided self-service UI.
acceptance_criteria: Given I open the policy authoring screen, when I enter policy details, then the form renders fields and scoping selectors (company, jurisdiction, location, department, group, employment type) driven by configuration.; Given I select scoping attributes, when I fill the form, then the UI validates required fields and reflects the resulting applicability before I save.; Given effective dating and versioning, when I author a change, then the screen lets me set the effective date and shows the version being created; Given my role permissions, when I access the screen, then only actions permitted to my role are enabled.
source: FR 6.10.1 (L4 presentation: authoring UI), FR 6.10.4

title: Browse and search applicable policies in a self-service library
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want a self-service policy library where I can browse and search the policies applicable to me, so that I can quickly find the rules relevant to my work.
acceptance_criteria: Given I open the policy library, when it loads, then I see only the in-force policies applicable to my role and scope.; Given many policies, when I search or filter by keyword or category, then the library returns matching policies within my visibility scope.; Given a policy has multiple versions, when I open it, then I see the version currently in force for me in a readable presentation; Given a restricted policy, when I search, then it is never surfaced in my results.
source: FR 6.10.4 (L4 presentation: self-service policy library, search/navigation)

title: Filter the policy list by active and inactive status
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to filter the policy document list by active policies versus inactive policies, so that I can focus on the policies currently in force or review those that have been retired.
acceptance_criteria: Given the policy document list, when I select the Active policies filter and display, then only policies within their effective period are listed.; Given the policy document list, when I select the Inactive policies filter and display, then only retired or out-of-period policies are listed.; Given a status filter is applied, when a policy's effective window changes such that its status changes, then it moves between the active and inactive filtered views accordingly; Given no filter is selected, when I display, then the full policy list is returned.
source: Kensium: Organization > Policy Document (Active policies / Inactive policies filter, Display)

title: Search policies by name and reset the search
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to search the policy documents by name and reset the filters, so that I can quickly locate a specific policy and then start a fresh search cleanly.
acceptance_criteria: Given many policy documents exist, when I enter a policy document name and click Search, then only policies whose name matches the term are listed.; Given I have applied a name search and status or date filters, when I click Reset, then all filters clear and the full unfiltered policy list is shown.; Given a search returns no matches, when the list renders, then an empty-result state is shown without error; Given a search term, when I combine it with the active/inactive filter, then results honor both the name match and the selected status.
source: Kensium: Organization > Policy Document (Search, Reset)

title: Exclude specific position levels from a policy
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to exclude specific position levels from a policy's applicability, so that senior or exempt roles are not governed by a policy that otherwise applies to their scope.
acceptance_criteria: Given a policy scoped to a population, when I designate one or more excluded position levels, then workers at those position levels do not inherit the policy even though they match the other scope attributes.; Given a policy with excluded position levels, when applicability is evaluated for a worker at an excluded level, then the policy is not applied to them.; Given the policy list, when I view a policy, then its excluded position level(s) are displayed alongside its applicability; Given I remove an exclusion, when applicability is re-evaluated, then the previously excluded position level again inherits the policy.
source: Kensium: Organization > Policy Document (Excluded position level(s) column)

title: Set an effective-from and effective-till validity window per policy
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to set both an effective-from and an effective-till date on a policy document, so that a policy is automatically active only within its valid window and expires without manual retirement.
acceptance_criteria: Given I author a policy document, when I set an effective-from date and an effective-till date, then the policy is treated as active only between those two dates inclusive.; Given a policy whose effective-till date has passed, when the active list is displayed, then the policy is classified as inactive automatically.; Given a policy with only an effective-from date and no effective-till, when it is evaluated, then it remains active indefinitely from its start date; Given an effective-till date earlier than the effective-from date, when I save, then the system blocks the save and prompts to correct the range.
source: Kensium: Organization > Policy Document (Effective from / Effective till columns)

title: Attach the policy document file to a policy record
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to upload and attach the actual policy document file (such as an Employee Manual PDF) to the policy record, so that employees can open and read the authoritative document rather than only metadata.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am authoring a policy document, when I upload a file such as a PDF or DOCX, then the file is stored and linked to that policy version.; Given a policy has an attached file, when a user opens the policy, then they can view or download the attached document.; Given a new policy version is created, when I attach an updated file, then the prior version retains its original file for audit while the new version references the new file; Given an unsupported or oversized file, when I attempt to upload, then the system rejects it with a clear message.
source: Kensium: Organization > Policy Document (Add new policy document, View/Preview of document)

title: Author a policy through a guided multi-step wizard
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to add a new policy document through a guided multi-step wizard with Next and Cancel controls, so that I can enter policy details, applicability, exclusions, and dates in a structured sequence.
acceptance_criteria: Given I click Add new policy document, when the wizard opens, then I progress through steps for policy details, applicability scope, excluded position levels, and effective dating using the Next control.; Given I am on a wizard step with missing required fields, when I click Next, then the wizard blocks progression and prompts for the required information.; Given I am partway through the wizard, when I click Cancel, then the draft is discarded and no policy is created; Given I complete all steps, when I finish the wizard, then the policy document is saved and appears in the list.
source: Kensium: Organization > Policy Document (Add new policy document wizard, Next / Cancel)

title: Preview a policy document before publishing
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to preview a policy document as it will appear to employees before I publish it, so that I can verify the content, applicability, and exclusions are correct.
acceptance_criteria: Given I have authored or edited a policy, when I click Preview, then the policy renders in the read-only form employees will see, including its content, applicability, and excluded position levels.; Given I am previewing an unpublished draft, when I review it, then the preview does not make the policy active or visible to employees.; Given the preview reveals an error, when I return to editing, then my in-progress changes are preserved; Given I confirm the preview is correct, when I publish, then the policy becomes active per its effective dates.
source: Kensium: Organization > Policy Document (Preview action)

title: Navigate a paginated policy list with total count
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want the policy document list to be paginated with a visible total count, so that I can efficiently browse a large catalog of policies across multiple pages.
acceptance_criteria: Given more policies exist than fit on one page, when the list loads, then it shows a page of items with a summary such as displaying items 1-10 of the total count.; Given a multi-page list, when I use next, last, or numbered page controls, then the corresponding page of policies is loaded.; Given active filters or a search are applied, when I paginate, then pagination operates over the filtered result set and the total count reflects the filter; Given I am on any page, when the list reloads, then the current page context and count remain consistent.
source: Kensium: Organization > Policy Document (pagination controls, Displaying items 1-10 of 73)

title: Refresh the policy document list
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to refresh the policy document list on demand, so that I see the latest policies and status changes without reloading the whole screen.
acceptance_criteria: Given the policy list is displayed, when I click Refresh, then the list reloads with the current set of policies and their latest effective status.; Given another admin has just added or retired a policy, when I refresh, then that change is reflected in my list; Given filters or a search are applied, when I refresh, then the refreshed list preserves my current filter and search context.
source: Kensium: Organization > Policy Document (Refresh)

title: View policy applicability and excluded position levels from the list
user_story: As an Employee (User), I want each policy in the list to show its applicability and excluded position levels, so that I can understand at a glance which policies govern me and which exclude my level.
acceptance_criteria: Given the policy list, when it renders, then each policy row shows its applicability and any excluded position level(s).; Given a policy that excludes my position level, when I review the list, then I can see that I am excluded from it; Given a policy applicable to me, when I open View or Preview, then I see its full content, applicability, and exclusions in read-only form; Given role-controlled visibility, when the list loads, then I see only policies my role and scope are permitted to view.
source: Kensium: Organization > Policy Document (Applicability and Excluded position level(s) columns, View / Preview)

title: Scope policies by position level
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want to scope a policy by position level in addition to the existing scope attributes, so that policies can target or exempt workers based on their seniority band.
acceptance_criteria: Given I am scoping a policy, when I select one or more position levels for applicability, then only workers at those position levels within the matched scope inherit the policy.; Given position-level scoping combined with exclusions, when applicability resolves, then included position levels apply and excluded position levels are removed deterministically.; Given a worker whose position level changes, when applicability is re-evaluated, then policies matching the new level are applied and those no longer matching are removed; Given a position-level-scoped policy, when I review applicability, then the system reflects the correct position-level mapping.
source: Kensium: Organization > Policy Document (position-level applicability and exclusion)

title: Track superseded policy versions by named edition
user_story: As a Company Admin, I want superseded policy editions to be retained under distinct named versions (such as Employee Manual 2024 and Employee Manual 2025), so that the historical lineage of a policy is clear and auditable.
acceptance_criteria: Given a policy is revised for a new period, when I create the new edition, then it is stored as a distinct named version with its own effective-from and effective-till dates while the prior edition is retained.; Given multiple editions of the same policy family, when I browse the list, then I can distinguish each edition by name and validity window; Given a superseded edition, when I open it, then its content and attached file are available read-only for reference; Given the current edition becomes effective, when applicability resolves, then the prior edition is no longer applied but remains in history.
source: Kensium: Organization > Policy Document (dated manual editions, e.g. Employee Manual 2024 vs 2025)
